The string reader has been disposed.
The reader is currently in use by a previous read operation.

member.name: T:WasmBox.Util.EscapeCharacters.StringExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for .

member.name: T:WasmBox.Util.Unindent.StringExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for .

member.name: M:WasmBox.Util.Unindent.StringExtensions.Unindent(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes indentation from the specified string.
member.param: The string from which to remove indentation.
The count of columns between tab stops. Must be a positive number.

returns.para: A string with content of , but unindented: the leading space, if any, common to all non-blank lines is removed from all lines (blank or not). This method also removes one leading line-ending and all trailing space from , if present.

Space is any mixture of the space (U+0020) and tab (U+0009) characters. Each tab advances to the next tab stop. Tab stops are spaces apart. If a tab in jumps past the indentation, this method replaces the tab with spaces to preserve alignment.
Lines end via any mixture of the carriage return (U+000D) and line feed (U+000A) characters. A line is blank if it contains no characters other than spaces or tabs.

member.name: T:WasmBox.Wasm.Interpret.ExecutionPolicy
member.summary: A description of an execution policy for WebAssembly modules.

member.name: M:WasmBox.Wasm.Interpret.ExecutionPolicy.Create(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new execution policy.
member.param: The maximal depth of the call stack.
The maximum size of any memory, in page units. A value of zero indicates that there is not maximum memory size.
Tells if memory access alignments should be taken to be normative instead of as hints.
Tells if CLR exceptions should be translated to values.

member.name: P:WasmBox.Wasm.Interpret.ExecutionPolicy.EnforceAlignment
member.summary: Tells if the alignment specified by memory instructions is to be taken as a mandatory alignment to which memory accesses must adhere instead of a mere hint.
member.value: true if unaligned accesses must throw exceptions; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: The WebAssembly specification states that memory instruction alignments do not affect execution semantics. In order to comply with the standard, this property should be set to false (the default).

member.name: P:WasmBox.Wasm.Interpret.ExecutionPolicy.MaxCallStackDepth
member.summary: Gets the maximal depth of the call stack.

member.name: P:WasmBox.Wasm.Interpret.ExecutionPolicy.MaxMemorySize
member.summary: Gets the maximum size of any memory, in page units. A value of zero indicates that there is not maximum memory size.

member.name: P:WasmBox.Wasm.Interpret.ExecutionPolicy.TranslateExceptions
member.summary: Tells if CLR exceptions should be translated to values.
member.value: true if WebAssembly execution should throw only values; false if it may also throw other types of exceptions.

member.summary: Gets the S-expression's tail: a sequence of S-expressions that trail the S-expression's head. Note that this tail may be empty even for S-expressions that are calls.

member.summary: Tells if this S-expression represents a single identifier token.

member.summary: Tests if this S-expression is a call to a keyword with a particular name.
